title: Branches as movable refs deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Branches as movable refs; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Branches as movable refs: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? A branch contains its own copied files and commits rather than naming one position in a shared commit graph.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Reason from references and commit ancestry: branch names move as commits are created, while snapshots belong to commit objects shared across the graph.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Reason from references and commit ancestry: branch names move as commits are created, while snapshots belong to commit objects shared across the graph.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Branches as movable refs instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.
